Within Akita Prefecture, aside from the main shrine, branches are enshrined at {{ill|Koshio Shrine|ja|古四王神社|lt=Akita City Jinaichi|lt_ja=秋田市寺内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshio Shrine|qq=}}, in Nikaho City, Ogata Town, and in Kazuno City, Hachimantai. According to the ancient history researcher {{ill|Niino Naokichi|ja|新野直吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niino Naokichi|qq=}}, it is speculated that the deity of Akitaura mentioned in the Nihon Shoki under the section on Emperor Saimei made contact with Abe Hirafu, who led an expedition to the north, and subsequently merged with the Hokuriku god of Koshi. Moreover, drawing on the myth of {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} subjugating Hokuriku, many visit the main shrine seeking success in competitive endeavors. == Cultural Properties == Within the shrine precincts, the main hall, the offertory hall, and the worship hall are the principal structures, with the main hall designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == Architectural Overview == "Preservation and Repair Records of the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine Main Hall" (compiled by the Cultural Property Construction Preservation Technology Association) * Designation Category: Important Cultural Property * Name = Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture) | Native Name: 古四王神社本殿 (Koshio Shrine Main Hall) * Number of Structures: 1 building * Designation Date: April 23, 1908 (Meiji 41) * Construction Date: 1570 (Genka Year 1) * Structural Style: Single-bay shrine Irimoya style, with side entrance, one-bay worship hall, karahafu (curved gable) style, thatched with cypress bark * Scale: Horizontal beam span 2.545 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|lt_ja=ｍ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}, rafter span 2.545 m == Characteristics of the Main Hall == Created in the late Muromachi period, the main hall employs materials such as cedar, pine, cypress, and chestnut in appropriate locations. It is an eclectic structure that integrates elements of traditional Japanese style, Zen (Tang) style, and Great Buddha (Indian) style, while also incorporating local regional characteristics. Detailed features include carvings of wisteria arabesque on the side of the worship hall and the one-piece wooden construction of the {{ill|Giboshi|ja|擬宝珠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giboshi|qq=}}, among other refined techniques.'Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine' P7–13 Furthermore, whereas most shrines typically face south or east, Koshio Shrine often faces north, and its veneration has been linked to martial deities—with some suggesting a connection to ancient Ezo rule. At the time, Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}} (professor at Tokyo Imperial University), an authority on art and architecture, exclaimed, “A rarity among rarities, an oddity among oddities,” and later the architectural historian {{ill|Amenuma Shun'ichi|ja|天沼俊一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenuma Shun'ichi|qq=}} (professor at Kyoto Imperial University) also praised it as “a world-class building transcending Japanese, Chinese, and Indian styles.”'Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine' P13 == Events == The regular Shinto ceremonies include the annual festival on September 8, door-opening on May 8, and door-closing on December 8, with New Year’s events held from December 31 through New Year’s Day. During the annual festival, kagura (sacred dance), lion dance, sword dance, and the dedication of Bonten are performed, along with the display and light-up of the main hall. In addition, in January—coinciding with Cultural Property Fire Prevention Day—the Koshio Shrine Cultural Property Fire Prevention Day and Koshio Hall Firefighting Mochi Festival are held. During the fire prevention drills, local firefighters and students from Higashi-Omagari Elementary School actively participate, followed by events during the mochi festival such as rice planting in the snow, Tenpitsu-yaki, and mochi pounding. == Timeline == * 1570 (Genka Year 1) – Establishment of the Koshio Shrine Main Hall. * 1873 (Meiji 6) – Becomes a village shrine. * 1905 (Meiji 38) – Investigation by Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}}, commissioned by the Ministry of Education’s Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Association. * April 23, 1908 (Meiji 41) – Designated as a “Special Protected Building” (equivalent to an “Important Cultural Property” under the Cultural Properties Protection Law) based on the Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law (Notification No. 43 of the Ministry of Home Affairs); on December 17, Suwa Shrine, Mizuhanome Shrine, and Inari Shrine are merged. * July 1930 (Showa 5) – Main hall dismantled and repaired (with new Tamagaki and Heiden constructed). * 1935 (Showa 10) – Investigation by Dr. {{ill|Amenuma Shun'ichi|ja|天沼俊一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenuma Shun'ichi|qq=}}, professor at Kyoto University. * October 1955 (Showa 30) – Major roof repairs. * 1966 (Showa 41) – Designated as a Shrine of Divine Food and Cloth Offerings. * July 1982 (Showa 57) – Fire disaster prevention facility work on the main hall. * November 1983 (Showa 58) – Repairs to the main hall’s protective fence. * November 1986 (Showa 61) – Major roof repairs of the main hall. * 2002 (Heisei 14) – Preservation and repair of the main hall. * January 2021 (Reiwa 3) – Torii gate collapses due to strong winds (reconstructed in July).[https://www.sakigake.jp/news/article/20210711AK0014/ Koshio Shrine Torii in Daisen, reinforced foundation and reconstruction after January’s strong winds] - Akita Sakigake Shinpō, viewed July 12, 2021. == Major Cultural Properties and Tourism in Daisen City == * {{ill|Former Ikeda Family Garden|ja|旧池田氏庭園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Ikeda Family Garden|qq=}} (National {{ill|Scenic Site|ja|名勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Site|qq=}}) * {{ill|Haruta Sakuato|ja|払田柵跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruta Sakuato|qq=}} (National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|3=史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}) * {{ill|Former Hongō Family Residence|ja|旧本郷家住宅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Hongō Family Residence|qq=}} ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}) == References == * Report on the Preservation and Repair Work of the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine Main Hall, compiled by the Cultural Property Construction Preservation Technology Association, Koshio Shrine, 2003 * Kyokyokai and Daisen City, “Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine”, 2007 * [http://www.city.daisen.akita.jp/docs/2013110600112/ Daisen City Official Website – Nationally Designated Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Building) “Koshio Shrine Main Hall”] == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} == External Links == * [http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/01_jinja/daisen_oomagari/16_kosiou.html Koshio Shrine] – Akita Prefecture Shrine Agency * [https://www.akitafan.com/archive/tourism/330 Koshio Shrine (こしおうじんじゃ)] – Akita Prefecture Tourism Federation * [http://www.city.daisen.akita.jp/docs/2013110600112/ Nationally Designated Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Building) “Koshio Shrine Main Hall”] – Daisen City, Akita Prefecture {{Shinto Navigation}} {{citation needed|ja|古四王神社|version=103544830|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Daisen City]] [[Category:History of Daisen City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Muromachi-period Architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture)]] [[ja:古四王神社]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] revision.comment: /* History */
revision.text:
{{複数の問題 |出典の明記=2020th year 1月30日 (木) 04:59 (UTC) |wikify=2020th year 1月30日 (木) 04:59 (UTC) }} {{infobox religious building | name = Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture) | native_name = 古四王神社 | address = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen City|qq=}}大曲字古四王際30 | 祭神 = {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}
{{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Tatemikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミズハノメ|lt=Water Wave Goddess|lt_ja=水波女神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | image = [[File:古四王神社本殿.jpg|thumb|古四王神社本殿]] | 例祭 = September 8 | 社格 = Shrine of Divine Food and Cloth Offerings | 創建 = 伝 {{ill|1570|ja|1570|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} ({{ill|Genka|ja|元亀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genka|qq=}} Year 1) | 本殿 = Single-bay shrine Irimoya style | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 39 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 = 17.1 | 経度度 = 140 | 経度分 = 29 | 経度秒 = 57.0 | 地図 = Japan Akita }} '''Koshio Shrine''' (古四王神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen City|qq=}} 大曲字古四王際. The main hall has been designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == History == According to old documents of the Togashi family, the head of the parish, it is said that the main hall of Koshio Shrine was established in {{ill|1570|ja|1570|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} ({{ill|Genka|ja|元亀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genka|qq=}} Year 1) when the feudal lord {{ill|Tozawa Clan|ja|戸沢氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tozawa Clan|qq=}} appointed the Peacock Castle lord {{ill|Togashi Clan|ja|冨樫氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Clan|qq=}} (冨樫左衛門太郎勝家) as magistrate. In 1930 (Showa 5), during dismantling and repair carried out by the {{ill|Ministry of Education|ja|文部省|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education|qq=}} (now the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology), an ink inscription reading “古川村　大工　甚兵衛” was discovered among the roof’s structural elements, revealing that the work was by the carpenter Jinbei from Hida, Hida City in present-day Gifu Prefecture.財団法人文化財建造物保存技術協会編『重要文化財　古四王神社本殿保存修理工事報告書』P4 In 1905 (Meiji 38), an investigation was conducted by Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}} (professor at Tokyo Imperial University) commissioned by the Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Association of the {{ill|Ministry of Education|ja|文部省|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education|qq=}}, and in 1908 (Meiji 41) the main hall was designated as a “Special Protected Building” (equivalent to an “Important Cultural Property” under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}}) based on the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law|qq=}}. This was the first cultural property designated building in Akita Prefecture. [[ja:古四王神社 (秋田県大仙市)]] == Deities and Origins of the Shrine == The enshrined deities are recorded in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|lt=Records of Ancient Matters|lt_ja=『古事記』|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|lt=Chronicles of Japan|lt_ja=『日本書紀』|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, and include {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (Ōhiko-no-Mikoto), who is said to have subdued the Hokuriku region, along with five other deities ({{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tatemikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミズハノメ|lt=Water Wave Goddess|lt_ja=水波女神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}}). Shrines named “Koshio” are common along the Sea of Japan coast from the Chūbu region northward, and examples can also be written as “胡四王”, “腰王”, or “故将”. Several theories have been proposed regarding its name: * The “Kōshi King Theory” holds that the enshrined {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} was the king of Koshi (the old name for the Hokuriku region). * The “Shitennō-ji Theory” suggests that the four guardian deities of Shitennō-ji—a temple which, through syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism, served as the protector of Akita Castle—later became corrupted into the name “Koshio.” The origin of the main shrine is based on the Kōshi King Theory. Within Akita Prefecture, aside from the main shrine, branches are enshrined at {{ill|Koshio Shrine|ja|古四王神社|lt=Akita City Jinaichi|lt_ja=秋田市寺内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshio Shrine|qq=}}, in Nikaho City, Ogata Town, and in Kazuno City, Hachimantai. According to the ancient history researcher {{ill|Niino Naokichi|ja|新野直吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niino Naokichi|qq=}}, it is speculated that the deity of Akitaura mentioned in the Nihon Shoki under the section on Emperor Saimei made contact with Abe Hirafu, who led an expedition to the north, and subsequently merged with the Hokuriku god of Koshi. Moreover, drawing on the myth of {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} subjugating Hokuriku, many visit the main shrine seeking success in competitive endeavors. == Cultural Properties == Within the shrine precincts, the main hall, the offertory hall, and the worship hall are the principal structures, with the main hall designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == Architectural Overview == "Preservation and Repair Records of the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine Main Hall" (compiled by the Cultural Property Construction Preservation Technology Association) * Designation Category: Important Cultural Property * Name = Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture) | Native Name: 古四王神社本殿 (Koshio Shrine Main Hall) * Number of Structures: 1 building * Designation Date: April 23, 1908 (Meiji 41) * Construction Date: 1570 (Genka Year 1) * Structural Style: Single-bay shrine Irimoya style, with side entrance, one-bay worship hall, karahafu (curved gable) style, thatched with cypress bark * Scale: Horizontal beam span 2.545 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|lt_ja=ｍ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}, rafter span 2.545 m == Characteristics of the Main Hall == Created in the late Muromachi period, the main hall employs materials such as cedar, pine, cypress, and chestnut in appropriate locations. It is an eclectic structure that integrates elements of traditional Japanese style, Zen (Tang) style, and Great Buddha (Indian) style, while also incorporating local regional characteristics. Detailed features include carvings of wisteria arabesque on the side of the worship hall and the one-piece wooden construction of the {{ill|Giboshi|ja|擬宝珠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giboshi|qq=}}, among other refined techniques.'Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine' P7–13 Furthermore, whereas most shrines typically face south or east, Koshio Shrine often faces north, and its veneration has been linked to martial deities—with some suggesting a connection to ancient Ezo rule. At the time, Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}} (professor at Tokyo Imperial University), an authority on art and architecture, exclaimed, “A rarity among rarities, an oddity among oddities,” and later the architectural historian {{ill|Amenuma Shun'ichi|ja|天沼俊一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenuma Shun'ichi|qq=}} (professor at Kyoto Imperial University) also praised it as “a world-class building transcending Japanese, Chinese, and Indian styles.”'Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine' P13 == Events == The regular Shinto ceremonies include the annual festival on September 8, door-opening on May 8, and door-closing on December 8, with New Year’s events held from December 31 through New Year’s Day. During the annual festival, kagura (sacred dance), lion dance, sword dance, and the dedication of Bonten are performed, along with the display and light-up of the main hall. In addition, in January—coinciding with Cultural Property Fire Prevention Day—the Koshio Shrine Cultural Property Fire Prevention Day and Koshio Hall Firefighting Mochi Festival are held. During the fire prevention drills, local firefighters and students from Higashi-Omagari Elementary School actively participate, followed by events during the mochi festival such as rice planting in the snow, Tenpitsu-yaki, and mochi pounding. == Timeline == * 1570 (Genka Year 1) – Establishment of the Koshio Shrine Main Hall. * 1873 (Meiji 6) – Becomes a village shrine. * 1905 (Meiji 38) – Investigation by Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}}, commissioned by the Ministry of Education’s Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Association. * April 23, 1908 (Meiji 41) – Designated as a “Special Protected Building” (equivalent to an “Important Cultural Property” under the Cultural Properties Protection Law) based on the Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law (Notification No. 43 of the Ministry of Home Affairs); on December 17, Suwa Shrine, Mizuhanome Shrine, and Inari Shrine are merged. * July 1930 (Showa 5) – Main hall dismantled and repaired (with new Tamagaki and Heiden constructed). * 1935 (Showa 10) – Investigation by Dr. {{ill|Amenuma Shun'ichi|ja|天沼俊一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenuma Shun'ichi|qq=}}, professor at Kyoto University. * October 1955 (Showa 30) – Major roof repairs. * 1966 (Showa 41) – Designated as a Shrine of Divine Food and Cloth Offerings. * July 1982 (Showa 57) – Fire disaster prevention facility work on the main hall. * November 1983 (Showa 58) – Repairs to the main hall’s protective fence. * November 1986 (Showa 61) – Major roof repairs of the main hall. * 2002 (Heisei 14) – Preservation and repair of the main hall. * January 2021 (Reiwa 3) – Torii gate collapses due to strong winds (reconstructed in July).[https://www.sakigake.jp/news/article/20210711AK0014/ Koshio Shrine Torii in Daisen, reinforced foundation and reconstruction after January’s strong winds] - Akita Sakigake Shinpō, viewed July 12, 2021. == Major Cultural Properties and Tourism in Daisen City == * {{ill|Former Ikeda Family Garden|ja|旧池田氏庭園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Ikeda Family Garden|qq=}} (National {{ill|Scenic Site|ja|名勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Site|qq=}}) * {{ill|Haruta Sakuato|ja|払田柵跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruta Sakuato|qq=}} (National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|3=史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}) * {{ill|Former Hongō Family Residence|ja|旧本郷家住宅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Hongō Family Residence|qq=}} ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}) == References == * Report on the Preservation and Repair Work of the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine Main Hall, compiled by the Cultural Property Construction Preservation Technology Association, Koshio Shrine, 2003 * Kyokyokai and Daisen City, “Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine”, 2007 * [http://www.city.daisen.akita.jp/docs/2013110600112/ Daisen City Official Website – Nationally Designated Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Building) “Koshio Shrine Main Hall”] == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} == External Links == * [http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/01_jinja/daisen_oomagari/16_kosiou.html Koshio revision.text:
{{複数の問題 |出典の明記=2020th year 1月30日 (木) 04:59 (UTC) |wikify=2020th year 1月30日 (木) 04:59 (UTC) }} {{infobox religious building | name = Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture) | native_name = 古四王神社 | address = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen City|qq=}}大曲字古四王際30 | 祭神 = {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}
{{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Tatemikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミズハノメ|lt=Water Wave Goddess|lt_ja=水波女神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | image = [[File:古四王神社本殿.jpg|thumb|古四王神社本殿]] | 例祭 = September 8 | 社格 = Shrine of Divine Food and Cloth Offerings | 創建 = 伝 {{ill|1570|ja|1570|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} ({{ill|Genka|ja|元亀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genka|qq=}} Year 1) | 本殿 = Single-bay shrine Irimoya style | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 39 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 = 17.1 | 経度度 = 140 | 経度分 = 29 | 経度秒 = 57.0 | 地図 = Japan Akita }} '''Koshio Shrine''' (古四王神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen City|qq=}} 大曲字古四王際. The main hall has been designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == History == According to old documents of the Togashi family, the head of the parish, it is said that the main hall of Koshio Shrine was established in {{ill|1570|ja|1570|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} ({{ill|Genka|ja|元亀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genka|qq=}} Year 1) when the feudal lord {{ill|Tozawa Clan|ja|戸沢氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tozawa Clan|qq=}} appointed the Peacock Castle lord {{ill|Togashi Clan|ja|冨樫氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Clan|qq=}} (冨樫左衛門太郎勝家) as magistrate. In 1930 (Showa 5), during dismantling and repair carried out by the {{ill|Ministry of Education|ja|文部省|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education|qq=}} (now the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology), an ink inscription reading “古川村　大工　甚兵衛” was discovered among the roof’s structural elements, revealing that the work was by the carpenter Jinbei from Hida, Hida City in present-day Gifu Prefecture.財団法人文化財建造物保存技術協会編『重要文化財　古四王神社本殿保存修理工事報告書』P4 In 1905 (Meiji 38), an investigation was conducted by Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}} (professor at Tokyo Imperial University) commissioned by the Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Association of the {{ill|Ministry of Education|ja|文部省|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education|qq=}}, and in 1908 (Meiji 41) the main hall was designated as a “Special Protected Building” (equivalent to an “Important Cultural Property” under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}}) based on the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law|qq=}}. This was the first cultural property designated building in Akita Prefecture. [[ja:古四王神社 (秋田県大仙市)]] == Deities and Origins of the Shrine == The enshrined deities are recorded in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|lt=Records of Ancient Matters|lt_ja=『古事記』|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|lt=Chronicles of Japan|lt_ja=『日本書紀』|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, and include {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (Ōhiko-no-Mikoto), who is said to have subdued the Hokuriku region, along with five other deities ({{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tatemikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミズハノメ|lt=Water Wave Goddess|lt_ja=水波女神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}}). Shrines named “Koshio” are common along the Sea of Japan coast from the Chūbu region northward, and examples can also be written as “胡四王”, “腰王”, or “故将”. Several theories have been proposed regarding its name: * The “Kōshi King Theory” holds that the enshrined {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} was the king of Koshi (the old name for the Hokuriku region). * The “Shitennō-ji Theory” suggests that the four guardian deities of Shitennō-ji—a temple which, through syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism, served as the protector of Akita Castle—later became corrupted into the name “Koshio.” The origin of the main shrine is based on the Kōshi King Theory. Within Akita Prefecture, aside from the main shrine, branches are enshrined at {{ill|Koshio Shrine|ja|古四王神社|lt=Akita City Jinaichi|lt_ja=秋田市寺内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshio Shrine|qq=}}, in Nikaho City, Ogata Town, and in Kazuno City, Hachimantai. According to the ancient history researcher {{ill|Niino Naokichi|ja|新野直吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niino Naokichi|qq=}}, it is speculated that the deity of Akitaura mentioned in the Nihon Shoki under the section on Emperor Saimei made contact with Abe Hirafu, who led an expedition to the north, and subsequently merged with the Hokuriku god of Koshi. Moreover, drawing on the myth of {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} subjugating Hokuriku, many visit the main shrine seeking success in competitive endeavors. == Cultural Properties == Within the shrine precincts, the main hall, the offertory hall, and the worship hall are the principal structures, with the main hall designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == Architectural Overview == "Preservation and Repair Records of the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine Main Hall" (compiled by the Cultural Property Construction Preservation Technology Association) * Designation Category: Important Cultural Property * Name = Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture) | Native Name: 古四王神社本殿 (Koshio Shrine Main Hall) * Number of Structures: 1 building * Designation Date: April 23, 1908 (Meiji 41) * Construction Date: 1570 (Genka Year 1) * Structural Style: Single-bay shrine Irimoya style, with side entrance, one-bay worship hall, karahafu (curved gable) style, thatched with cypress bark * Scale: Horizontal beam span 2.545 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|lt_ja=ｍ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}, rafter span 2.545 m == Characteristics of the Main Hall == Created in the late Muromachi period, the main hall employs materials such as cedar, pine, cypress, and chestnut in appropriate locations. It is an eclectic structure that integrates elements of traditional Japanese style, Zen (Tang) style, and Great Buddha (Indian) style, while also incorporating local regional characteristics. Detailed features include carvings of wisteria arabesque on the side of the worship hall and the one-piece wooden construction of the {{ill|Giboshi|ja|擬宝珠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giboshi|qq=}}, among other refined techniques.'Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine' P7–13 Furthermore, whereas most shrines typically face south or east, Koshio Shrine often faces north, and its veneration has been linked to martial deities—with some suggesting a connection to ancient Ezo rule. At the time, Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}} (professor at Tokyo Imperial University), an authority on art and architecture, exclaimed, “A rarity among rarities, an oddity among oddities,” and later the architectural historian {{ill|Amenuma Shun'ichi|ja|天沼俊一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenuma Shun'ichi|qq=}} (professor at Kyoto Imperial University) also praised it as “a world-class building transcending Japanese, Chinese, and Indian styles.”'Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine' P13 == Events == The regular Shinto ceremonies include the annual festival on September 8, door-opening on May 8, and door-closing on December 8, with New Year’s events held from December 31 through New Year’s Day. During the annual festival, kagura (sacred dance), lion dance, sword dance, and the dedication of Bonten are performed, along with the display and light-up of the main hall. In addition, in January—coinciding with Cultural Property Fire Prevention Day—the Koshio Shrine Cultural Property Fire Prevention Day and Koshio Hall Firefighting Mochi Festival are held. During the fire prevention drills, local firefighters and students from Higashi-Omagari Elementary School actively participate, followed by events during the mochi festival such as rice planting in the snow, Tenpitsu-yaki, and mochi pounding. == Timeline == * 1570 (Genka Year 1) – Establishment of the Koshio Shrine Main Hall. * 1873 (Meiji 6) – Becomes a village shrine. * 1905 (Meiji 38) – Investigation by Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}}, commissioned by the Ministry of Education’s Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Association. * April 23, 1908 (Meiji 41) – Designated as a “Special Protected Building” (equivalent to an “Important Cultural Property” under the Cultural Properties Protection Law) based on the Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law (Notification No. 43 of the Ministry of Home Affairs); on December 17, Suwa Shrine, Mizuhanome Shrine, and Inari Shrine are merged. * July 1930 (Showa 5) – Main hall dismantled and repaired (with new Tamagaki and Heiden constructed). * 1935 (Showa 10) – Investigation by Dr. {{ill|Amenuma Shun'ichi|ja|天沼俊一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenuma Shun'ichi|qq=}}, professor at Kyoto University. * October 1955 (Showa 30) – Major roof repairs. * 1966 (Showa 41) – Designated as a Shrine of Divine Food and Cloth Offerings. * July 1982 (Showa 57) – Fire disaster prevention facility work on the main hall. * November 1983 (Showa 58) – Repairs to the main hall’s protective fence. * November 1986 (Showa 61) – Major roof repairs of the main hall. * 2002 (Heisei 14) – Preservation and repair of the main hall. * January 2021 (Reiwa 3) – Torii gate collapses due to strong winds (reconstructed in July).[https://www.sakigake.jp/news/article/20210711AK0014/ Koshio Shrine Torii in Daisen, reinforced foundation and reconstruction after January’s strong winds] - Akita Sakigake Shinpō, viewed July 12, 2021. == Major Cultural Properties and Tourism in Daisen City == * {{ill|Former Ikeda Family Garden|ja|旧池田氏庭園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Ikeda Family Garden|qq=}} (National {{ill|Scenic Site|ja|名勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Site|qq=}}) * {{ill|Haruta Sakuato|ja|払田柵跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruta Sakuato|qq=}} (National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|3=史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}) * {{ill|Former Hongō Family Residence|ja|旧本郷家住宅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Hongō Family Residence|qq=}} ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}) == References == * Report on the Preservation and Repair Work of the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine Main Hall, compiled by the Cultural Property Construction Preservation Technology Association, Koshio Shrine, 2003 * Kyokyokai and Daisen City, “Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine”, 2007 * [http://www.city.daisen.akita.jp/docs/2013110600112/ Daisen City Official Website – Nationally Designated Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Building) “Koshio Shrine Main Hall”] == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} == External Links == * [http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/01_jinja/daisen_oomagari/16_kosiou.html Koshio Shrine] – Akita Prefecture Shrine Agency * [https://www.akitafan.com/archive/tourism/330 Koshio Shrine (こしおうじんじゃ)] – Akita Prefecture Tourism Federation * [http://www.city.daisen.akita.jp/docs/2013110600112/ Nationally Designated Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Building) “Koshio Shrine Main Hall”] – Daisen City, Akita Prefecture {{Shinto Navigation}} {{citation needed|ja|古四王神社|version=103544830|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Daisen City]] [[Category:History of Daisen City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Muromachi-period Architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture)]] [[ja:古四王神社]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture)]][[Category:qq]]

Within Akita Prefecture, aside from the main shrine, branches are enshrined at {{ill|Koshio Shrine|ja|古四王神社|lt=Akita City Jinaichi|lt_ja=秋田市寺内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshio Shrine|qq=}}, in Nikaho City, Ogata Town, and in Kazuno City, Hachimantai. According to the ancient history researcher {{ill|Niino Naokichi|ja|新野直吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niino Naokichi|qq=}}, it is speculated that the deity of Akitaura mentioned in the Nihon Shoki under the section on Emperor Saimei made contact with Abe Hirafu, who led an expedition to the north, and subsequently merged with the Hokuriku god of Koshi. Moreover, drawing on the myth of {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} subjugating Hokuriku, many visit the main shrine seeking success in competitive endeavors. == Cultural Properties == Within the shrine precincts, the main hall, the offertory hall, and the worship hall are the principal structures, with the main hall designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == Architectural Overview == "Preservation and Repair Records of the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine Main Hall" (compiled by the Cultural Property Construction Preservation Technology Association) * Designation Category: Important Cultural Property * Name = Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture) | Native Name: 古四王神社本殿 (Koshio Shrine Main Hall) * Number of Structures: 1 building * Designation Date: April 23, 1908 (Meiji 41) * Construction Date: 1570 (Genka Year 1) * Structural Style: Single-bay shrine Irimoya style, with side entrance, one-bay worship hall, karahafu (curved gable) style, thatched with cypress bark * Scale: Horizontal beam span 2.545 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|lt_ja=ｍ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}, rafter span 2.545 m == Characteristics of the Main Hall == Created in the late Muromachi period, the main hall employs materials such as cedar, pine, cypress, and chestnut in appropriate locations. It is an eclectic structure that integrates elements of traditional Japanese style, Zen (Tang) style, and Great Buddha (Indian) style, while also incorporating local regional characteristics. Detailed features include carvings of wisteria arabesque on the side of the worship hall and the one-piece wooden construction of the {{ill|Giboshi|ja|擬宝珠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giboshi|qq=}}, among other refined techniques.'Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine' P7–13 Furthermore, whereas most shrines typically face south or east, Koshio Shrine often faces north, and its veneration has been linked to martial deities—with some suggesting a connection to ancient Ezo rule. At the time, Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}} (professor at Tokyo Imperial University), an authority on art and architecture, exclaimed, “A rarity among rarities, an oddity among oddities,” and later the architectural historian {{ill|Amenuma Shun'ichi|ja|天沼俊一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenuma Shun'ichi|qq=}} (professor at Kyoto Imperial University) also praised it as “a world-class building transcending Japanese, Chinese, and Indian styles.”'Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine' P13 == Events == The regular Shinto ceremonies include the annual festival on September 8, door-opening on May 8, and door-closing on December 8, with New Year’s events held from December 31 through New Year’s Day. During the annual festival, kagura (sacred dance), lion dance, sword dance, and the dedication of Bonten are performed, along with the display and light-up of the main hall. In addition, in January—coinciding with Cultural Property Fire Prevention Day—the Koshio Shrine Cultural Property Fire Prevention Day and Koshio Hall Firefighting Mochi Festival are held. During the fire prevention drills, local firefighters and students from Higashi-Omagari Elementary School actively participate, followed by events during the mochi festival such as rice planting in the snow, Tenpitsu-yaki, and mochi pounding. == Timeline == * 1570 (Genka Year 1) – Establishment of the Koshio Shrine Main Hall. * 1873 (Meiji 6) – Becomes a village shrine. * 1905 (Meiji 38) – Investigation by Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}}, commissioned by the Ministry of Education’s Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Association. * April 23, 1908 (Meiji 41) – Designated as a “Special Protected Building” (equivalent to an “Important Cultural Property” under the Cultural Properties Protection Law) based on the Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law (Notification No. 43 of the Ministry of Home Affairs); on December 17, Suwa Shrine, Mizuhanome Shrine, and Inari Shrine are merged. * July 1930 (Showa 5) – Main hall dismantled and repaired (with new Tamagaki and Heiden constructed). * 1935 (Showa 10) – Investigation by Dr. {{ill|Amenuma Shun'ichi|ja|天沼俊一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenuma Shun'ichi|qq=}}, professor at Kyoto University. * October 1955 (Showa 30) – Major roof repairs. * 1966 (Showa 41) – Designated as a Shrine of Divine Food and Cloth Offerings. * July 1982 (Showa 57) – Fire disaster prevention facility work on the main hall. * November 1983 (Showa 58) – Repairs to the main hall’s protective fence. * November 1986 (Showa 61) – Major roof repairs of the main hall. * 2002 (Heisei 14) – Preservation and repair of the main hall. * January 2021 (Reiwa 3) – Torii gate collapses due to strong winds (reconstructed in July).[https://www.sakigake.jp/news/article/20210711AK0014/ Koshio Shrine Torii in Daisen, reinforced foundation and reconstruction after January’s strong winds] - Akita Sakigake Shinpō, viewed July 12, 2021. == Major Cultural Properties and Tourism in Daisen City == * {{ill|Former Ikeda Family Garden|ja|旧池田氏庭園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Ikeda Family Garden|qq=}} (National {{ill|Scenic Site|ja|名勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Site|qq=}}) * {{ill|Haruta Sakuato|ja|払田柵跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruta Sakuato|qq=}} (National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|3=史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}) * {{ill|Former Hongō Family Residence|ja|旧本郷家住宅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Hongō Family Residence|qq=}} ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}) == References == * Report on the Preservation and Repair Work of the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine Main Hall, compiled by the Cultural Property Construction Preservation Technology Association, Koshio Shrine, 2003 * Kyokyokai and Daisen City, “Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine”, 2007 * [http://www.city.daisen.akita.jp/docs/2013110600112/ Daisen City Official Website – Nationally Designated Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Building) “Koshio Shrine Main Hall”] == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} == External Links == * [http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/01_jinja/daisen_oomagari/16_kosiou.html Koshio Shrine] – Akita Prefecture Shrine Agency * [https://www.akitafan.com/archive/tourism/330 Koshio Shrine (こしおうじんじゃ)] – Akita Prefecture Tourism Federation * [http://www.city.daisen.akita.jp/docs/2013110600112/ Nationally Designated Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Building) “Koshio Shrine Main Hall”] – Daisen City, Akita Prefecture {{Shinto Navigation}} {{citation needed|ja|古四王神社|version=103544830|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Daisen City]] [[Category:History of Daisen City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Muromachi-period Architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture)]] [[ja:古四王神社]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture)]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|八坂刀売命|" to "|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|"
revision.text:
{{複数の問題 |出典の明記=2020th year 1月30日 (木) 04:59 (UTC) |wikify=2020th year 1月30日 (木) 04:59 (UTC) }} {{infobox religious building | name = Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture) | native_name = 古四王神社 | address = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen City|qq=}}大曲字古四王際30 | 祭神 = {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}
{{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Tatemikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミズハノメ|lt=Water Wave Goddess|lt_ja=水波女神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | image = [[File:古四王神社本殿.jpg|thumb|古四王神社本殿]] | 例祭 = September 8 | 社格 = Shrine of Divine Food and Cloth Offerings | 創建 = 伝 {{ill|1570|ja|1570|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} ({{ill|Genka|ja|元亀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genka|qq=}} Year 1) | 本殿 = Single-bay shrine Irimoya style | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 39 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 = 17.1 | 経度度 = 140 | 経度分 = 29 | 経度秒 = 57.0 | 地図 = Japan Akita }} '''Koshio Shrine''' (古四王神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen City|qq=}} 大曲字古四王際. The main hall has been designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == History == According to old documents of the Togashi family, the head of the parish, it is said that the main hall of Koshio Shrine was established in {{ill|1570|ja|1570|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} ({{ill|Genka|ja|元亀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genka|qq=}} Year 1) when the feudal lord {{ill|Tozawa Clan|ja|戸沢氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tozawa Clan|qq=}} appointed the Peacock Castle lord {{ill|Togashi Clan|ja|冨樫氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Clan|qq=}} (冨樫左衛門太郎勝家) as magistrate. In 1930 (Showa 5), during dismantling and repair carried out by the {{ill|Ministry of Education|ja|文部省|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education|qq=}} (now the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology), an ink inscription reading “古川村　大工　甚兵衛” was discovered among the roof’s structural elements, revealing that the work was by the carpenter Jinbei from Hida, Hida City in present-day Gifu Prefecture.財団法人文化財建造物保存技術協会編『重要文化財　古四王神社本殿保存修理工事報告書』P4 In 1905 (Meiji 38), an investigation was conducted by Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}} (professor at Tokyo Imperial University) commissioned by the Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Association of the {{ill|Ministry of Education|ja|文部省|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education|qq=}}, and in 1908 (Meiji 41) the main hall was designated as a “Special Protected Building” (equivalent to an “Important Cultural Property” under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}}) based on the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law|qq=}}. This was the first cultural property designated building in Akita Prefecture. [[ja:古四王神社 (秋田県大仙市)]] == Deities and Origins of the Shrine == The enshrined deities are recorded in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|lt=Records of Ancient Matters|lt_ja=『古事記』|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|lt=Chronicles of Japan|lt_ja=『日本書紀』|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, and include {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (Ōhiko-no-Mikoto), who is said to have subdued the Hokuriku region, along with five other deities ({{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tatemikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミズハノメ|lt=Water Wave Goddess|lt_ja=水波女神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}}). Shrines named “Koshio” are common along the Sea of Japan coast from the Chūbu region northward, and examples can also be written as “胡四王”, “腰王”, or “故将”. Several theories have been proposed regarding its name: * The “Kōshi King Theory” holds that the enshrined {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} was the king of Koshi (the old name for the Hokuriku region). * The “Shitennō-ji Theory” suggests that the four guardian deities of Shitennō-ji—a temple which, through syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism, served as the protector of Akita Castle—later became corrupted into the name “Koshio.” The origin of the main shrine is based on the Kōshi King Theory. Within Akita Prefecture, aside from the main shrine, branches are enshrined at {{ill|Koshio Shrine|ja|古四王神社|lt=Akita City Jinaichi|lt_ja=秋田市寺内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshio Shrine|qq=}}, in Nikaho City, Ogata Town, and in Kazuno City, Hachimantai. According to the ancient history researcher {{ill|Niino Naokichi|ja|新野直吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niino Naokichi|qq=}}, it is speculated that the deity of Akitaura mentioned in the Nihon Shoki under the section on Emperor Saimei made contact with Abe Hirafu, who led an expedition to the north, and subsequently merged with the Hokuriku god of Koshi. Moreover, drawing on the myth of {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} subjugating Hokuriku, many visit the main shrine seeking success in competitive endeavors. == Cultural Properties == Within the shrine precincts, the main hall, the offertory hall, and the worship hall are the principal structures, with the main hall designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == Architectural Overview == "Preservation and Repair Records of the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine Main Hall" (compiled by the Cultural Property Construction Preservation Technology Association) * Designation Category: Important Cultural Property * Name = Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture) | Native Name: 古四王神社本殿 (Koshio Shrine Main Hall) * Number of Structures: 1 building * Designation Date: April 23, 1908 (Meiji 41) * Construction Date: 1570 (Genka Year 1) * Structural Style: Single-bay shrine Irimoya style, with side entrance, one-bay worship hall, karahafu (curved gable) style, thatched with cypress bark * Scale: Horizontal beam span 2.545 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|lt_ja=ｍ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}, rafter span 2.545 m == Characteristics of the Main Hall == Created in the late Muromachi period, the main hall employs materials such as cedar, pine, cypress, and chestnut in appropriate locations. It is an eclectic structure that integrates elements of traditional Japanese style, Zen (Tang) style, and Great Buddha (Indian) style, while also incorporating local regional characteristics. Detailed features include carvings of wisteria arabesque on the side of the worship hall and the one-piece wooden construction of the {{ill|Giboshi|ja|擬宝珠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giboshi|qq=}}, among other refined techniques.'Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine' P7–13 Furthermore, whereas most shrines typically face south or east, Koshio Shrine often faces north, and its veneration has been linked to martial deities—with some suggesting a connection to ancient Ezo rule. At the time, Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}} (professor at Tokyo Imperial University), an authority on art and architecture, exclaimed, “A rarity among rarities, an oddity among oddities,” and later the architectural historian {{ill|Amenuma Shun'ichi|ja|天沼俊一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenuma Shun'ichi|qq=}} (professor at Kyoto Imperial University) also praised it as “a world-class building transcending Japanese, Chinese, and Indian styles.”'Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine' P13 == Events == The regular Shinto ceremonies include the annual festival on September 8, door-opening on May 8, and door-closing on December 8, with New Year’s events held from December 31 through New Year’s Day. During the annual festival, kagura (sacred dance), lion dance, sword dance, and the dedication of Bonten are performed, along with the display and light-up of the main hall. In addition, in January—coinciding with Cultural Property Fire Prevention Day—the Koshio Shrine Cultural Property Fire Prevention Day and Koshio Hall Firefighting Mochi Festival are held. During the fire prevention drills, local firefighters and students from Higashi-Omagari Elementary School actively participate, followed by events during the mochi festival such as rice planting in the snow, Tenpitsu-yaki, and mochi pounding. == Timeline == * 1570 (Genka Year 1) – Establishment of the Koshio Shrine Main Hall. * 1873 (Meiji 6) – Becomes a village shrine. * 1905 (Meiji 38) – Investigation by Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}}, commissioned by the Ministry of Education’s Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Association. * April 23, 1908 (Meiji 41) – Designated as a “Special Protected Building” (equivalent to an “Important Cultural Property” under the Cultural Properties Protection Law) based on the Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law (Notification No. 43 of the Ministry of Home Affairs); on December 17, Suwa Shrine, Mizuhanome Shrine, and Inari Shrine are merged. * July 1930 (Showa 5) – Main hall dismantled and repaired (with new Tamagaki and Heiden constructed). * 1935 (Showa 10) – Investigation by Dr. {{ill|Amenuma Shun'ichi|ja|天沼俊一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenuma Shun'ichi|qq=}}, professor at Kyoto University. * October 1955 (Showa 30) – Major roof repairs. * 1966 (Showa 41) – Designated as a Shrine of Divine Food and Cloth Offerings. * July 1982 (Showa 57) – Fire disaster prevention facility work on the main hall. * November 1983 (Showa 58) – Repairs to the main hall’s protective fence. * November 1986 (Showa 61) – Major roof repairs of the main hall. * 2002 (Heisei 14) – Preservation and repair of the main hall. * January 2021 (Reiwa 3) – Torii gate collapses due to strong winds (reconstructed in July).[https://www.sakigake.jp/news/article/20210711AK0014/ Koshio Shrine Torii in Daisen, reinforced foundation and reconstruction after January’s strong winds] - Akita Sakigake Shinpō, viewed July 12, 2021. == Major Cultural Properties and Tourism in Daisen City == * {{ill|Former Ikeda Family Garden|ja|旧池田氏庭園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Ikeda Family Garden|qq=}} (National {{ill|Scenic Site|ja|名勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Site|qq=}}) * {{ill|Haruta Sakuato|ja|払田柵跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruta Sakuato|qq=}} (National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|3=史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}) * {{ill|Former Hongō Family Residence|ja|旧本郷家住宅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Hongō Family Residence|qq=}} ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}) == References == * Report on the Preservation and Repair Work of the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine Main Hall, compiled by the Cultural Property Construction Preservation Technology Association, Koshio Shrine, 2003 * Kyokyokai and Daisen City, “Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine”, 2007 * [http://www.city.daisen.akita.jp/docs/2013110600112/ Daisen City Official Website – Nationally Designated Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Building) “Koshio revision.text:
{{複数の問題 |出典の明記=2020年1月30日 (木) 04:59 (UTC) |wikify=2020年1月30日 (木) 04:59 (UTC) }} {{神社 | 名称=古四王神社 | 所在地=[[秋田県]][[大仙市]]大曲字古四王際30 | 祭神=[[大彦命]]
[[天照皇大神]]
[[豊受大神]]
[[建御名方命]]
[[八坂刀売命]]
[[ミズハノメ|水波女神]] | 画像=[[File:古四王神社本殿.jpg|thumb|古四王神社本殿]] | 例祭=9月8日 | 社格=神饌幣帛料供進神社 | 創建=伝[[1570年]]（[[元亀]]元年） | 本殿=一間社入母屋造 | 位置= | 緯度度=39 | 緯度分=26 | 緯度秒=17.1 | 経度度=140 | 経度分=29 | 経度秒=57.0 | 地図=Japan Akita }} '''古四王神社'''（こしおうじんじゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[大仙市]]大曲字古四王際にある神社。本殿は[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 歴史 == 古四王神社本殿は、氏子総代の冨樫家の古文書によると、1570年（元亀元年） に領主[[戸沢氏]]が、孔雀城主である冨樫氏（冨樫左衛門太郎勝家）を奉行として建立したと伝えられる。1930年（昭和5年）に行われた文部省（現文部科学省）による解体修理の際に、軒の組物の中に「古川村 大工 甚兵衛」という墨書が発見され、現在の岐阜県飛騨市出身の大工・甚兵衛の作であることが判明した財団法人文化財建造物保存技術協会編『重要文化財　古四王神社本殿保存修理工事報告書』P4。古四王神社本殿は1905年（明治38年）、[[文部省]]古社寺保存会嘱託で工学博士の[[伊東忠太]]（東京帝国大学教授）の調査を経て、1908年（明治41年）に当時の[[古社寺保存法]]に基づく「特別保護建造物」（[[文化財保護法]]下の「重要文化財」に相当）に指定された。秋田県では初の文化財指定建造物である。 == 祭神と神社の由来 == 祭神は[[古事記|『古事記』]]・[[日本書紀|『日本書紀』]]で北陸を平定したとされる[[大彦命]]（オオビコノミコト）と他五柱（ 天照皇大神、豊受大神、建御名方命、八坂刀売命、水波女神）。コシオウという神社は、中部以北の日本海側に多く見られ、「胡四王」、「腰王」、「故将」などと書く社の例もあり、その由来については以下の説がある。 * 祭神である大彦命が高志（越）国（現在の北陸地方）の王であったからという高志王説。 * 秋田城を鎮護する四天王寺が神仏習合して鎮守となった四王堂の四王が、古四王に転訛したという四天王寺説などがある。 本社の由来は高志王説に基づく。 秋田県内では本社の他に、[[古四王神社|秋田市寺内]]、にかほ市象潟町、鹿角市八幡平に鎮座する。古代史研究家の[[新野直吉]]によると、『日本書紀』斉明天皇条にみえる秋田浦の神が北方遠征を行った阿倍比羅夫と接触し，北陸の神高志（越）王と結合したと推測している『世界大百科事典』「古四王」の項。また、祭神の一つである大彦命の北陸平定神話から、本社には勝負事の利益を求めて参詣する人もいる。 == 文化財 == 境内の主要な建物として本殿、幣殿、拝殿があり、本殿が重要文化財に指定されている。 === 建築概要=== 「重要文化財　古四王神社本殿保存修理記録」（財団法人文化財建造物保存技術協会編『重要文化財　古四王神社本殿保存修理工事報告書』） * 指定区分：重要文化財 * 名称：古四王神社本殿（こしおうじんじゃほんでん） * 員数：1棟 * 指定年月日：1908年（明治41年）4月23日 * 建築年代：1570年（元亀元年） * 構造形式：一間社入母屋造、妻入、向拝一間、唐破風造、こけら葺 * 規模：桁行2.545[[メートル|ｍ]]、梁行2.545m === 本殿の特色 === 室町時代末期の作で、材質は杉・松・桧・栗などが適所に使用されている。和様・禅宗様（唐様）・大仏様（天竺様）などの各様式を採り入れた折衷様で、地方色も採り入れているのが特徴である。向拝の側面上にある藤唐草の彫刻や、木目を利用した一木造りの[[擬宝珠]]など、細部に様々な手法が施されている『地域の宝　重要文化財　古四王神社　市民の勉強会から生まれたボランティアガイド・マニュアル』P7〜13。また、通常神社は南か東を向いているものが多いが、古四王神社は北を向いている場合が多く、古四王神社崇敬については、武神との関わりから、古代の蝦夷支配との関係を指摘する声もある。当時、美術建築の権威であった伊東忠太（東京帝国大学教授）は「奇中の奇、珍中の珍」と感嘆し、後に建築史家の[[天沼俊一]]（京都帝国大学教授）も「和(日本)・唐(中国)・天(インド)を超越した天下一品の建物」と絶賛した『地域の宝　重要文化財　古四王神社　市民の勉強会から生まれたボランティアガイド・マニュアル』P13。 == 行事 == 恒例の神事は9月8日（例大祭）、5月8日（戸開）と12月8日（戸閉）があり、12月31日から元旦にかけて正月行事が行われる。例大祭では神楽、獅子舞、刀舞、梵天の奉納が行われるほか、本殿の御開帳やライトアップが行われる。また、文化財防火デーに因んで1月には古四王神社文化財防火デー・古四王堂火消しもちまつりが行われる。防火訓練では、地元の消防団の他、東大曲小学校児童らも活躍し、続いて行われる火消しもちまつりでは、雪中田植え、天筆焼、もちつきが行われる。 == 年表 == * 1570年（元亀元年） - 古四王神社本殿建立。 * 1873年（明治6年） - 村社となる。 * 1905年（明治38年） - 文部省（現文部科学省）古社寺保存会嘱託伊東忠太（東京帝国大学教授）工学博士調査。 * 1908年（明治41年）4月23日 - 古社寺保存法に基づき「特別保護建造物」（文化財保護法下の「重要文化財」に相当）に指定（明治41年内務省告示第43号）。12月17日　諏訪神社、水波女神社、稲荷神社を合併する。 * 1930年（昭和5年）7月 - 本殿解体修理（玉垣・幣殿新設）。 * 1935年（昭和10年） - 京都大学教授　天沼俊一博士調査。 * 1955年（昭和30年）10月 - 屋根大修理。 * 1966年（昭和41年） - 神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定。 * 1982年（昭和57年）7月 - 本殿防災施設工事。 * 1983年（昭和58年）11月 - 本殿防護柵修理。 * 1986年（昭和61年）11月 - 本殿屋根大修理。 * 2002年（平成14年） - 本殿保存修理。 * 2021年（令和3年）1月 - 強風で鳥居が倒壊（7月に再建）[https://www.sakigake.jp/news/article/20210711AK0014/ 大仙・古四王神社鳥居、基礎強化し再建へ1月の強風で倒壊] - 秋田魁新報 2021年7月12日閲覧。。 == 大仙市の主要文化財・観光 == *[[旧池田氏庭園]]（国の[[名勝]]） *[[払田柵跡]]（国の[[史跡]]） *[[旧本郷家住宅]]（[[登録有形文化財]]） == 参考資料 == * 財団法人文化財建造物保存技術協会編『重要文化財　古四王神社本殿保存修理工事報告書』古四王神社　2003年 *曲陽会・大仙市編『地域の宝　重要文化財　古四王神社　市民の勉強会から生まれたボランティアガイド・マニュアル』2007年 * [http://www.city.daisen.akita.jp/docs/2013110600112/ 大仙市HP　国指定重要有形文化財（建造物）「古四王神社本殿」] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} == 外部リンク == * [http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/01_jinja/daisen_oomagari/16_kosiou.html 古四王神社]秋田県神社庁 * [https://www.akitafan.com/archive/tourism/330 古四王神社（こしおうじんじゃ）]秋田県観光連盟 * [http://www.city.daisen.akita.jp/docs/2013110600112/ 国指定重要有形文化財（建造物）「古四王神社本殿」]秋田県大仙市 {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:こしおうしんしや}} [[Category:秋田県の神社]] [[Category:大仙市の建築物]] [[Category:大仙市の歴史]] [[Category:秋田県の重要文化財]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]]

{{複数の問題 |出典の明記=2020th year 1月30日 (木) 04:59 (UTC) |wikify=2020th year 1月30日 (木) 04:59 (UTC) }} {{infobox religious building | name = Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture) | native_name = 古四王神社 | address = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen City|qq=}}大曲字古四王際30 | 祭神 = {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}
{{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Tatemikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミズハノメ|lt=Water Wave Goddess|lt_ja=水波女神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | image = [[File:古四王神社本殿.jpg|thumb|古四王神社本殿]] | 例祭 = September 8 | 社格 = Shrine of Divine Food and Cloth Offerings | 創建 = 伝 {{ill|1570|ja|1570|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} ({{ill|Genka|ja|元亀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genka|qq=}} Year 1) | 本殿 = Single-bay shrine Irimoya style | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 39 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 = 17.1 | 経度度 = 140 | 経度分 = 29 | 経度秒 = 57.0 | 地図 = Japan Akita }} '''Koshio Shrine''' (古四王神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen City|qq=}} 大曲字古四王際. The main hall has been designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == History == According to old documents of the Togashi family, the head of the parish, it is said that the main hall of Koshio Shrine was established in {{ill|1570|ja|1570|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} ({{ill|Genka|ja|元亀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genka|qq=}} Year 1) when the feudal lord {{ill|Tozawa Clan|ja|戸沢氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tozawa Clan|qq=}} appointed the Peacock Castle lord {{ill|Togashi Clan|ja|冨樫氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togashi Clan|qq=}} (冨樫左衛門太郎勝家) as magistrate. In 1930 (Showa 5), during dismantling and repair carried out by the {{ill|Ministry of Education|ja|文部省|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education|qq=}} (now the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology), an ink inscription reading “古川村　大工　甚兵衛” was discovered among the roof’s structural elements, revealing that the work was by the carpenter Jinbei from Hida, Hida City in present-day Gifu Prefecture.財団法人文化財建造物保存技術協会編『重要文化財　古四王神社本殿保存修理工事報告書』P4 In 1905 (Meiji 38), an investigation was conducted by Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}} (professor at Tokyo Imperial University) commissioned by the Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Association of the {{ill|Ministry of Education|ja|文部省|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Education|qq=}}, and in 1908 (Meiji 41) the main hall was designated as a “Special Protected Building” (equivalent to an “Important Cultural Property” under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}}) based on the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law|qq=}}. This was the first cultural property designated building in Akita Prefecture. [[ja:古四王神社 (秋田県大仙市)]] == Deities and Origins of the Shrine == The enshrined deities are recorded in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|lt=Records of Ancient Matters|lt_ja=『古事記』|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|lt=Chronicles of Japan|lt_ja=『日本書紀』|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, and include {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (Ōhiko-no-Mikoto), who is said to have subdued the Hokuriku region, along with five other deities ({{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tatemikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Tomachinomikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミズハノメ|lt=Water Wave Goddess|lt_ja=水波女神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}}). Shrines named “Koshio” are common along the Sea of Japan coast from the Chūbu region northward, and examples can also be written as “胡四王”, “腰王”, or “故将”. Several theories have been proposed regarding its name: * The “Kōshi King Theory” holds that the enshrined {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} was the king of Koshi (the old name for the Hokuriku region). * The “Shitennō-ji Theory” suggests that the four guardian deities of Shitennō-ji—a temple which, through syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism, served as the protector of Akita Castle—later became corrupted into the name “Koshio.” The origin of the main shrine is based on the Kōshi King Theory. Within Akita Prefecture, aside from the main shrine, branches are enshrined at {{ill|Koshio Shrine|ja|古四王神社|lt=Akita City Jinaichi|lt_ja=秋田市寺内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshio Shrine|qq=}}, in Nikaho City, Ogata Town, and in Kazuno City, Hachimantai. According to the ancient history researcher {{ill|Niino Naokichi|ja|新野直吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niino Naokichi|qq=}}, it is speculated that the deity of Akitaura mentioned in the Nihon Shoki under the section on Emperor Saimei made contact with Abe Hirafu, who led an expedition to the north, and subsequently merged with the Hokuriku god of Koshi. Moreover, drawing on the myth of {{ill|Oohiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} subjugating Hokuriku, many visit the main shrine seeking success in competitive endeavors. == Cultural Properties == Within the shrine precincts, the main hall, the offertory hall, and the worship hall are the principal structures, with the main hall designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == Architectural Overview == "Preservation and Repair Records of the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine Main Hall" (compiled by the Cultural Property Construction Preservation Technology Association) * Designation Category: Important Cultural Property * Name = Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture) | Native Name: 古四王神社本殿 (Koshio Shrine Main Hall) * Number of Structures: 1 building * Designation Date: April 23, 1908 (Meiji 41) * Construction Date: 1570 (Genka Year 1) * Structural Style: Single-bay shrine Irimoya style, with side entrance, one-bay worship hall, karahafu (curved gable) style, thatched with cypress bark * Scale: Horizontal beam span 2.545 {{ill|meter|ja|メートル|lt=m|lt_ja=ｍ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meter|qq=}}, rafter span 2.545 m == Characteristics of the Main Hall == Created in the late Muromachi period, the main hall employs materials such as cedar, pine, cypress, and chestnut in appropriate locations. It is an eclectic structure that integrates elements of traditional Japanese style, Zen (Tang) style, and Great Buddha (Indian) style, while also incorporating local regional characteristics. Detailed features include carvings of wisteria arabesque on the side of the worship hall and the one-piece wooden construction of the {{ill|Giboshi|ja|擬宝珠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giboshi|qq=}}, among other refined techniques.'Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine' P7–13 Furthermore, whereas most shrines typically face south or east, Koshio Shrine often faces north, and its veneration has been linked to martial deities—with some suggesting a connection to ancient Ezo rule. At the time, Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}} (professor at Tokyo Imperial University), an authority on art and architecture, exclaimed, “A rarity among rarities, an oddity among oddities,” and later the architectural historian {{ill|Amenuma Shun'ichi|ja|天沼俊一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenuma Shun'ichi|qq=}} (professor at Kyoto Imperial University) also praised it as “a world-class building transcending Japanese, Chinese, and Indian styles.”'Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine' P13 == Events == The regular Shinto ceremonies include the annual festival on September 8, door-opening on May 8, and door-closing on December 8, with New Year’s events held from December 31 through New Year’s Day. During the annual festival, kagura (sacred dance), lion dance, sword dance, and the dedication of Bonten are performed, along with the display and light-up of the main hall. In addition, in January—coinciding with Cultural Property Fire Prevention Day—the Koshio Shrine Cultural Property Fire Prevention Day and Koshio Hall Firefighting Mochi Festival are held. During the fire prevention drills, local firefighters and students from Higashi-Omagari Elementary School actively participate, followed by events during the mochi festival such as rice planting in the snow, Tenpitsu-yaki, and mochi pounding. == Timeline == * 1570 (Genka Year 1) – Establishment of the Koshio Shrine Main Hall. * 1873 (Meiji 6) – Becomes a village shrine. * 1905 (Meiji 38) – Investigation by Dr. {{ill|Itō Chūtā|ja|伊東忠太|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Chūtā|qq=}}, commissioned by the Ministry of Education’s Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Association. * April 23, 1908 (Meiji 41) – Designated as a “Special Protected Building” (equivalent to an “Important Cultural Property” under the Cultural Properties Protection Law) based on the Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law (Notification No. 43 of the Ministry of Home Affairs); on December 17, Suwa Shrine, Mizuhanome Shrine, and Inari Shrine are merged. * July 1930 (Showa 5) – Main hall dismantled and repaired (with new Tamagaki and Heiden constructed). * 1935 (Showa 10) – Investigation by Dr. {{ill|Amenuma Shun'ichi|ja|天沼俊一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenuma Shun'ichi|qq=}}, professor at Kyoto University. * October 1955 (Showa 30) – Major roof repairs. * 1966 (Showa 41) – Designated as a Shrine of Divine Food and Cloth Offerings. * July 1982 (Showa 57) – Fire disaster prevention facility work on the main hall. * November 1983 (Showa 58) – Repairs to the main hall’s protective fence. * November 1986 (Showa 61) – Major roof repairs of the main hall. * 2002 (Heisei 14) – Preservation and repair of the main hall. * January 2021 (Reiwa 3) – Torii gate collapses due to strong winds (reconstructed in July).[https://www.sakigake.jp/news/article/20210711AK0014/ Koshio Shrine Torii in Daisen, reinforced foundation and reconstruction after January’s strong winds] - Akita Sakigake Shinpō, viewed July 12, 2021. == Major Cultural Properties and Tourism in Daisen City == * {{ill|Former Ikeda Family Garden|ja|旧池田氏庭園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Ikeda Family Garden|qq=}} (National {{ill|Scenic Site|ja|名勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scenic Site|qq=}}) * {{ill|Haruta Sakuato|ja|払田柵跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruta Sakuato|qq=}} (National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|3=史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}) * {{ill|Former Hongō Family Residence|ja|旧本郷家住宅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Hongō Family Residence|qq=}} ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}) == References == * Report on the Preservation and Repair Work of the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine Main Hall, compiled by the Cultural Property Construction Preservation Technology Association, Koshio Shrine, 2003 * Kyokyokai and Daisen City, “Regional Treasure: Volunteer Guide Manual Born from a Citizen Study Group on the Important Cultural Property, Koshio Shrine”, 2007 * [http://www.city.daisen.akita.jp/docs/2013110600112/ Daisen City Official Website – Nationally Designated Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Building) “Koshio Shrine Main Hall”] == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} == External Links == * [http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/01_jinja/daisen_oomagari/16_kosiou.html Koshio Shrine] – Akita Prefecture Shrine Agency * [https://www.akitafan.com/archive/tourism/330 Koshio Shrine (こしおうじんじゃ)] – Akita Prefecture Tourism Federation * [http://www.city.daisen.akita.jp/docs/2013110600112/ Nationally Designated Registered Tangible Cultural Property (Building) “Koshio Shrine Main Hall”] – Daisen City, Akita Prefecture {{Shinto Navigation}} {{citation needed|ja|古四王神社|version=103544830|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Daisen City]] [[Category:History of Daisen City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Muromachi-period Architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture)]] [[ja:古四王神社]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koshio Shrine (Daisen City, Akita Prefecture)]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|ja|古四王神社 (秋田県大仙市)|version=97729838|comment=Imported full ja history}}
